mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL values indicate accelerating inflation, which forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances. This persistent inflation pressure compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive direction in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack increases significantly. This sudden deterioration in employment conditions triggers immediate risk-off sentiment among equity investors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 70% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in GDPC1 and HOUST signals robust aggregate demand across both production and housing sectors. This dual demand surge drives corporate earnings growth expectations upward, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in over 60% of triggered quarters, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints exacerbated by higher borrowing costs. This supply contraction limits output growth and pressures equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 65% of cases, or the regime-conditional predictive accuracy drops below baseline levels.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS drops below 3.0%, it signals an aggressive monetary easing phase. This rapid policy pivot reduces discount rates and liquidity premiums, triggering immediate bullish momentum that yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 60% of months where FEDFUNDS crosses below 3.0%, or the threshold signal exhibits a Rank IC below 0.02 in rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in PAYEMS and a decline in UNRATE confirms a tightening labor market with expanding payrolls and shrinking slack. This dual labor strength supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative 60-day returns in over 55% of observed months, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample forecast accuracy relative to single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS values indicate strengthening consumer spending and retail demand. This sustained increase in final sales to households directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 65% of months, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO exceeds 105.0, it signals an overheating industrial sector operating above historical capacity norms. This supply bottleneck triggers inflationary fears and prompts anticipatory risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in over 70% of months where INDPRO surpasses 105.0, or the threshold trigger shows no predictive edge (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward evaluation.
